status: Fixed in validate fast/full split lane
finding: Routine closeback evidence can spend about five minutes in `npm run validate:cli` during a single task evidence pass, with the operator UI showing a long-running shell step rather than a compact validator phase breakdown, cache hit/miss explanation, or task-scope-based fast path. A related closeback contract mismatch can also mark `validate:cli` and `validate:git-head-evidence` as advisory/out-of-scope in pre-close, then fail `taskflow close --write` because the closure packet still requires those entries. This makes Captain closeback loops feel stalled and undermines trust in pre-close guidance.
expectedBehavior: ATM evidence runs should expose validator phase timing, reuse fresh task-compatible evidence where safe, and derive the smallest required validator set from task scope and protected-surface impact. Long validators should stream or report progress and offer resumable/status commands so operators can distinguish healthy long work from a stuck run. Pre-close, auto-evidence, closure-packet generation, and close-write gates must agree on which validators are blocking versus advisory.
evidenceOrRepro: Dogfood during 2026-07-11 Team Agents closeback: while refreshing evidence for `TASK-TEAM-0051`, the sequential evidence loop reached `RUNNING: npm run validate:cli` and remained active for roughly 4m29s to nearly five minutes before completion. During `TASK-TEAM-0052`, pre-close reported `validate:cli` and `validate:git-head-evidence` as `skipped-out-of-scope` advisory validators, but `taskflow close --write` then failed with `ATM_TASK_CLOSE_CLOSURE_PACKET_INVALID` missing `validationPasses/validate:cli` and `validationPasses/validate:git-head-evidence`. Fixed on 2026-07-11 in stages: `scripts/validate-cli.ts` emits stderr phase markers, 30-second heartbeat progress, per-focused-test timings, and final elapsed time. Independent focused subprocess tests run in parallel with stdout/stderr preserved on failure. Heavy historical-delivery coverage was moved into `tests/cli/validate-cli-historical-delivery.test.ts`, and git/hook-heavy regressions such as `git-commit-failure-residue-rollback.test.ts`, `git-commit-timeout-and-status.test.ts`, `git-record-commit.test.ts`, and `validate-cli-close-gate.test.ts` remain direct targeted tests instead of being run concurrently inside the full integration lane, because local dogfood showed they amplify Windows/Git branch-queue contention when overlapped with the workspace fixture. The default `npm run validate:cli` runs a fast profile that exits before the heavy integration workspace; local verification after the final fast/full split passed in 10s, below the 30s target. `npm run validate:cli:full` retained the integration workspace and passed in 1m19s on 2026-07-11 after replacing repeated onboarding/ATMChart child-process launches with in-process CLI calls and adding full workspace subphase markers for onboarding/ATMChart, integration adapters, spec validation, bootstrap/verify, upgrade/review, and evidence quoting/auto-link. Targeted follow-up verification passed for `node --strip-types tests/cli/git-commit-failure-residue-rollback.test.ts`, `node --strip-types tests/cli/git-commit-timeout-and-status.test.ts`, `node --strip-types tests/cli/git-record-commit.test.ts`, and `node --strip-types tests/cli/validate-cli-close-gate.test.ts`. `docs/testing-strategy.md` sets the default `validate:cli` budget to under 30 seconds and documents that new spawned/temp-workspace regressions must be standalone focused tests registered in the appropriate parallel batch; `scripts/validate-cli.ts` carries the same authoring rule next to the focused-test registration point. `tasks close` closure-packet required gates now use the same changed-file filter as pre-close validator classification through `requiredValidationPassesForClosure(frameworkStatus.requiredGates, closePacketChangedFiles)`, with `tests/cli/closure-required-gates-contract.test.ts` wired into the parallel `validate:cli` batch to prevent regression. The validator resume regression now uses fast deterministic validators instead of the root-wide neutrality scanner, removing a parallel-batch flake.
followUp: Keep full-suite optimization as an ordinary future enhancement, not an open blocker for the default closeback lane: further work can cache unchanged full-workspace receipts or split the full integration workspace into additional standalone tests if full runtime becomes release-blocking.
